_comment_top: R6 Wave 7 (Q9 batch migration) — seed row for the TextRefinementHandler 'refine' method.
_comment_routing: Runtime invocation routes to the C# class via sprk_handlerclass = 'TextRefinementHandler'. The handler reads sprk_configuration.method = 'refine' to dispatch to the refine pipeline.
_comment_method_dispatch: Three rows (TEXT-REFINE / TEXT-KEYPOINTS / TEXT-SUMMARY) share one handler class. Each row's sprk_configuration.method discriminator selects the internal method. This preserves the pre-R6 three-tool LLM exposure while keeping a single handler.
_comment_consumption: Read by scripts/Seed-TypedHandlers.ps1. Idempotent — script queries by sprk_handlerclass + sprk_name 'SYS-%' filter; UPSERTs.
sprk_description: Reformat or improve the clarity of a text passage per a given instruction. Use when the user wants to rewrite, simplify, expand, reformat, or restyle content. Output is plain refined text — no preamble or commentary.
_comment_availableincontexts: 100000002 = Both (Playbook + Chat). Q9 batch migration: invocable from both playbook orchestration and chat-driven function calling.

sprk_jsonschema.title: TextRefinementHandler.RefineText Request
sprk_jsonschema.description: Argument shape for chat-driven invocation of the refine method. The 'text' field carries the content to refine; 'instruction' is the specific refinement directive.

text.description: The text passage to refine. The handler does NOT pre-process this — it is the literal input to the LLM.

instruction.description: The refinement instruction (e.g., 'rewrite in formal language', 'simplify this paragraph', 'convert to bullet points').

sprk_configuration._comment: method = 'refine' is the dispatch discriminator. This row exposes only RefineText.
